description_long: This sculpture depicts a winged human figure, likely an angel, rendered in a classical style. The figure is dressed in flowing, draped garments that gather at the waist and fall in naturalistic folds, suggesting movement. The wings are detailed and extend from the upper back, with one wing more prominently visible from certain angles. The facial features are softly defined, and the head is slightly tilted, giving the figure a contemplative or serene expression. The surface of the sculpture has a weathered, patinated appearance, indicating it is made of bronze or a similar metal. The base is irregular, suggesting it may have been designed to be mounted on a pedestal or integrated into a larger installation.
description: A weathered bronze sculpture of a winged human figure in flowing garments, with detailed wings and a serene expression.
description_view_0: The figure is seen in profile, highlighting the draped garments and the curvature of the wings extending from the back.
description_view_1: A rear view reveals both wings and the gathered folds of the garment, with the back and base clearly visible.
description_view_2: This side view emphasizes the back of the head, the wing structure, and the flowing lines of the robe.
description_view_3: A frontal angle shows the face, outstretched arm, and the dynamic posture of the figure, with one leg slightly forward.
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate installation environment.
